Title: Action-Figure-Me: Blister-Pack Collectible That Looks Store-Bought
Teaser: Turns anyone into a hyper-detailed toy-aisle action figure sealed in a retail blister pack — fake branding, molded accessories, and that glossy injection-molded sheen that makes it look photographed on a shelf.
PromptHint: Describe [PERSON] (look + vibe), list 2-4 [ACCESSORIES], and name the [BRAND/THEME] on the box.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer shooting toy-aisle packaging for DALL·E 3. TASK: Write ONE rich, self-contained natural-language scene — DALL·E 3 ignores parameters, weights, and flags (no --ar, no ::), so describe everything in plain prose. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal product shot, exactly one figure, no real trademarks, short box text only (DALL·E 3 renders 1-3 words cleanly, not paragraphs). THINK SILENTLY first: the figure's wardrobe, pose, and which accessories tell [PERSON]'s story; do not print this reasoning. THEN WRITE the scene: a 1/7-scale plastic action figure of [PERSON] standing inside a sealed retail blister pack against a printed cardboard backer, with realistic articulated joints and a glossy injection-molded finish; molded accessory bubbles beside it holding [ACCESSORIES]; the backer printed with the brand name '[BRAND/THEME]' in bold playful type, a barcode, and a small 'Ages 8+' badge. Specify a clean studio softbox key from upper-left, soft fill, a faint reflection on the plastic blister, and shallow depth of field keeping the figure tack-sharp while the white seamless background falls gently out of focus. Mood: nostalgic, premium, collectible; warm primary accents against neutral white. To preserve detail against DALL·E 3's auto-rewrite, open with 'I NEED a photorealistic image of:'. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly, nothing else: 1) PROMPT: <one detailed paragraph> 2) ONE-LINE TWEAK: <a follow-up sentence to paste in chat to swap the box color>.

Title: Disposable-Camera Realism: The Anti-'AI Look' Snapshot
Teaser: Recreates any memory as a gritty 2000s disposable-camera print — harsh flash, light leaks, off white balance, a timestamp and a white border — so it reads as a real photo, not a glossy AI render.
PromptHint: Describe the [MOMENT] (who/where/doing what); set the [DECADE]; add an optional [TIMESTAMP DATE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a film-photography art director writing one DALL·E 3 scene engineered to defeat the clean, over-lit 'AI look'. TASK: produce ONE flowing paragraph in plain prose (DALL·E 3 takes no flags or weights). CONSTRAINTS: candid and imperfect, single frame, no glossy retouching, realistic flash physics. THINK SILENTLY about why real snapshots feel real — harsh direct flash, slightly off white balance, a hand or hair caught mid-motion — then discard that reasoning and write only the scene. SCENE: a candid snapshot of [MOMENT], shot on a cheap [DECADE] disposable point-and-shoot with built-in flash; harsh on-camera light flattens faces and throws a hard shadow on the wall behind, highlights slightly blown, a warm-to-greenish color cast, visible film grain, and a subtle orange light leak bleeding into one corner. Frame it as a physical print with a thick white Polaroid-style border and a small orange digital timestamp reading '[TIMESTAMP DATE]' in the lower-right. Composition: casual, slightly tilted, subject off-center, grabbed in a hurry. Mood: nostalgic, spontaneous, lived-in. Open with 'I NEED a snapshot photo of:' to keep DALL·E 3 from over-polishing it. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly, nothing else: 1) PROMPT: <one paragraph> 2) GRITTIER VARIANT: <one short sentence pushing more grain and a stronger light leak>.
